## Description

A clear and concise description of the changes introduced by this pull request.

## Related Issues

Link any related issues that this PR addresses:

- Fixes #_(issue number)_
- Resolves #_(issue number)_
- Related to #_(issue number)_

## Type of Change

Mark the type of change with an `x`:

- [ ] :sparkles: **New feature** — non-breaking change that adds functionality
- [ ] :bug: **Bug fix** — non-breaking change that fixes an issue
- [ ] :boom: **Breaking change** — fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change
- [ ] :recycle: **Refactor** — code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature
- [ ] :zap: **Performance** — code change that improves performance
- [ ] :memo: **Documentation** — changes to documentation only
- [ ] :white_check_mark: **Tests** — adding or updating tests
- [ ] :wrench: **Chore** — build process, tooling, or auxiliary changes
- [ ] :lock: **Security** — fixes a security vulnerability

## How Has This Been Tested?

Describe the tests you ran to verify your changes. Provide instructions so reviewers can reproduce.

- [ ] Unit tests
- [ ] Integration tests
- [ ] Manual testing in development environment
- [ ] Manual testing in production build

**Test configuration**:
- Node.js version:
- Browser:
- Environment variables needed (if any):

## Checklist

Before submitting this PR, confirm that:

- [ ] My code follows the project's coding standards (TypeScript strict mode, Tailwind utilities, etc.)
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation
- [ ] My changes generate no new ESLint warnings
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [ ] New and existing unit tests pass locally with my changes
- [ ] Any dependent changes have been merged and published in downstream modules
- [ ] I have verified that no secrets, API keys, or credentials are included in this PR
